title: Enforce `noinline` at call sites
author: dingxiangfei2009
description:
_Related to #45808._

[LangRef](https://llvm.org/docs/LangRef.html#:~:text=to%20be%20generated.-,noinline,-This%20attribute%20indicates) literally promises that a call on a function annotated with noinline will be preserved.

If we take this interpretation, it entails that even when there is a rewrite opportunity given a `returned` argument, inlining should never happen. `returned` is just an optimisation hint at best and semantics always come first.

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

; Function Attrs: nounwind
define i32 @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) #0 {
entry:
  %ptr = load ptr, ptr %argv, align 8
  %0 = call i32 @compute(ptr %ptr, i32 %argc)
  %1 = icmp slt i32 %argc, 2
  br i1 %1, label %done, label %do_work

do_work:                                          ; preds = %entry
  %2 = icmp eq ptr %ptr, null
  br i1 %2, label %null, label %done

null:                                             ; preds = %do_work
  call void @call_if_null(ptr %ptr)
  br label %done

done:                                             ; preds = %null, %do_work, %entry
  %retval = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %0, %do_work ], [ %0, %null ]
  ret i32 %retval
}

; Function Attrs: noinline nounwind memory(read)
declare i32 @compute(ptr noundef nonnull, i32) #1

; Function Attrs: nounwind
declare void @call_if_null(ptr) #0

attributes #0 = { nounwind }
attributes #1 = { noinline nounwind memory(read) }

comments.author: llvmorg-github-actions[bot]
comments.body:
<!--LLVM PR SUMMARY COMMENT-->

@llvm/pr-subscribers-llvm-transforms

Author: wieDasDing (dingxiangfei2009)

<details>
<summary>Changes</summary>

[LangRef](https://llvm.org/docs/LangRef.html#:~:text=to%20be%20generated.-,noinline,-This%20attribute%20indicates) literally promises that a call on a function annotated with noinline will be preserved.

If we take this interpretation, it entails that even when there is a rewrite opportunity given a `returned` argument, inlining should never happen. `returned` is just an optimisation hint at best and semantics always come first.

---
Full diff: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/198128.diff


3 Files Affected:

- (modified) llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp (+1-1) 
- (added) llvm/test/Transforms/DCE/noinline-calls.ll (+54) 
- (modified) llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/unused-nonnull.ll (+4-3) 


``````````diff
diff --git a/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp b/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp
index a622e0248fce8..fd88ed6b3e1a8 100644
--- a/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp
+++ b/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp
@@ -4854,7 +4854,7 @@ Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::visitCallBase(CallBase &Call) {
     if (I) return eraseInstFromFunction(*I);
   }
 
-  if (!Call.use_empty() && !Call.isMustTailCall())
+  if (!Call.use_empty() && !Call.isMustTailCall() && !Call.isNoInline())
     if (Value *ReturnedArg = Call.getReturnedArgOperand()) {
       Type *CallTy = Call.getType();
       Type *RetArgTy = ReturnedArg->getType();
diff --git a/llvm/test/Transforms/DCE/noinline-calls.ll b/llvm/test/Transforms/DCE/noinline-calls.ll
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000..a223f9bffcbd0
--- /dev/null
+++ b/llvm/test/Transforms/DCE/noinline-calls.ll
@@ -0,0 +1,54 @@
+; NOTE: Assertions have been autogenerated by utils/update_test_checks.py
+; RUN: opt -S -passes=instcombine,sccp,dce < %s | FileCheck %s
+
+; Scenario 1: Returned attribute
+; A noinline function returns its argument.
+; The call instruction should be preserved, even though the return value can be
+; simplified by InstCombine.
+
+declare ptr @demo1(ptr returned) noinline memory(none)
+
+define ptr @foo1(ptr %arg) {
+; CHECK-LABEL: @foo1(
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CALL:%.*]] = call ptr @demo1(ptr [[ARG:%.*]])
+; CHECK-NEXT:    ret ptr [[CALL]]
+;
+  %call = call ptr @demo1(ptr %arg)
+  ret ptr %call
+}
+
+; Scenario 2: Constant return value
+; A noinline function returns a constant.
+; The call instruction should be preserved, even though the return value
+; is constant-foldable by SCCP.
+
+define internal ptr @demo2() noinline memory(none) {
+; CHECK-LABEL: @demo2(
+; CHECK-NEXT:    ret ptr null
+;
+  ret ptr null
+}
+
+define ptr @foo2() {
+; CHECK-LABEL: @foo2(
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CALL:%.*]] = call ptr @demo2()
+; CHECK-NEXT:    ret ptr [[CALL]]
+;
+  %call = call ptr @demo2()
+  ret ptr %call
+}
+
+; Scenario 3: Unused return value
+; A noinline function has no side effects, and its return value is unused.
+; The call instruction should be preserved by DCE.
+
+declare i32 @demo3() noinline memory(none)
+
+define void @foo3() {
+; CHECK-LABEL: @foo3(
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CALL:%.*]] = call i32 @demo3()
+; CHECK-NEXT:    ret void
+;
+  %call = call i32 @demo3()
+  ret void
+}
diff --git a/llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/unused-nonnull.ll b/llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/unused-nonnull.ll
index af468593b887c..3c0fd0c34a467 100644
--- a/llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/unused-nonnull.ll
+++ b/llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/unused-nonnull.ll
@@ -11,8 +11,9 @@ define i32 @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) #0 {
 ; CHECK-LABEL: define {{[^@]+}}@main
 ; CHECK-SAME: (i32 [[ARGC:%.*]], ptr readonly captures(none) [[ARGV:%.*]]) local_unnamed_addr #[[ATTR0:[0-9]+]] {
 ; CHECK-NEXT:  entry:
-; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP0:%.*]] = icmp slt i32 [[ARGC]], 2
-; CHECK-NEXT:    [[SPEC_SELECT:%.*]] = select i1 [[TMP0]], i32 0, i32 [[ARGC]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP0:%.*]] = tail call i32 @compute(ptr poison, i32 [[ARGC]])
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP1:%.*]] = icmp slt i32 [[ARGC]], 2
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[SPEC_SELECT:%.*]] = select i1 [[TMP1]], i32 0, i32 [[TMP0]]
 ; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 [[SPEC_SELECT]]
 ;
 entry:
@@ -36,7 +37,7 @@ done:
 
 define i32 @compute(ptr noundef nonnull %ptr, i32 %x) #1 {
 ; CHECK-LABEL: define {{[^@]+}}@compute
-; CHECK-SAME: (ptr noundef nonnull readnone captures(none) [[PTR:%.*]], i32 returned [[X:%.*]]) local_unnamed_addr #[[ATTR1:[0-9]+]] {
+; CHECK-SAME: (ptr nonnull readnone captures(none) [[PTR:%.*]], i32 returned [[X:%.*]]) local_unnamed_addr #[[ATTR1:[0-9]+]] {
 ; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 [[X]]
 ;
   ret i32 %x

``````````

</details>
